cards.definition: The moisture level of parchment coffee right after pulping and washing, before any drying has taken place.
cards.analogy: The starting weight before a long dehydration process — most of what gets 'lost' during drying is this water.
cards.why_it_matters: The baseline that makes the eventual drying target meaningful — without it, '11%' is just a number.
cards.cloze: Freshly pulped parchment coffee initially contains around ___% water content.

cards.definition: The moisture percentage parchment coffee should be dried down to before it's considered storage-ready.
cards.analogy: The target reading on a moisture meter that tells a processor 'stop drying now,' not a rough guess.
cards.why_it_matters: Under-drying risks mould in storage; over-drying wastes labor and can affect bean quality — this is the number both sides are measured against.
cards.cloze: Parchment coffee should be dried to a recommended moisture level of ___%.

cards.definition: The typical number of days needed to dry parchment coffee down to its target moisture, assuming it's spread correctly.
cards.analogy: A baking time, not an instant process — rushing it produces an uneven result.
cards.why_it_matters: A useful sanity check: a lot claiming to be dried in a couple of days, or left out for a month, is a flag either way.
cards.cloze: Parchment coffee generally takes about ___ days to dry when spread at proper thickness.

cards.definition: The specific point in the drying cycle when parchment coffee is most vulnerable to wrinkling if exposed to intense midday sun.
cards.analogy: A specific vulnerable stage in a longer process, the way a photo print is most fragile partway through developing, not at the very start or end.
cards.why_it_matters: A detail that separates careful drying-yard management from just 'spreading it out and hoping.'
cards.cloze: Parchment coffee should not be exposed to the hottest midday sun especially on the ___ day of drying, to avoid wrinkled beans.

cards.definition: The cup defect that results from storing or transporting coffee in new gunny bags that weren't aired out first.
cards.analogy: A brand-new cast iron pan tainting the first few meals cooked in it before it's been properly seasoned and cleaned.
cards.why_it_matters: A packaging detail worth asking about — it's a preventable defect that has nothing to do with the coffee itself.
cards.cloze: New gunny bags should be turned inside out and aerated before use, otherwise coffee absorbs natural oils and off odours, producing a(n) ___ cup.
